## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so our next strategy is positioning. And that is basically how the market sees you, what they see, your value or prestige. Think of like Chevy versus BMW in the automobile market. So Chevy here in the United States is sort of like every person's car, the just not super budget, I guess, but just not fancy whatsoever. Then you have BMW, which they're not even cars, they're the ultimate driving machine, right? And so BMW is pretty high end. And then of course there's up above BMW, you can go to Bentley or Rolls Royce or whatever. So these are different layers of positioning. Think about Toyota and Lexus, same car company really. Toyota wanted to create a premium brand. They wanted to go out, started creating premium cars, but they already had this positioning, sort of like Chevy, sort of like the every person's car. And so rather than trying to recreate their positioning and convincing people that now Toyotas are super high end, they created a new company, they called it Lexus. And so that's how they got that position is they created this new car and started making really high end cars called Lexus. It's a lot like that for anyone in any market, any kind of business. The people in your market, they wanna sort out, where are you? High end mid tier or, oops, high end mid tier low end. Where do you fit in? In general, in this market, I think you wanna be seen as a leader who provides massive value. That's the positioning I want. I think that's the positioning that I have. And I think it's a great positioning for you to pursue as well. You can be seen as a leader and be seen as someone who over delivers and provides massive value. And you don't wanna compete on price. And generally in this market, there's a bad thing to do. You wanna compete on value. So you're never gonna see me like going up and trying to be the lowest price course in any area. I wanna be like the highest price or among the highest price because I wanna provide a massive value. And when you do that, high end positioning is a great thing because it allows you to charge higher prices. And higher prices, well, I'll get to that in just a second. But the high end, there's so much goodness. I'll go through this. But the thing to remember is a well run product lunch, a well orchestrated product lunch absolutely maximizes your positioning in the market. It raises your stature in the market. And it does it pretty much instantly and almost automatically. Especially if you're doing a JV launch because if you do a joint venture launch and now you might have five or 10 joint venture partners and they're mailing their lists and they're talking about how awesome you are. I mean, that's the way to get massive group positioning right away, massive expert positioning almost immediately. When I came out with product launch formula,
